questions.question: What is the most dangerous outcome in sales?
questions.explanation: A 'Maybe' keeps you in limbo, wastes mental energy, and clogs your pipeline. A 'No' is clarity.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the 'Priority Question'?

options.text: To determine if they are ready to buy NOW or just 'exploring'
To see who the decision maker is

questions.explanation: You need to separate 'educating curious people' from 'selling to active buyers.' The Priority Question reveals urgency.

questions.question: If you ask 'What happens if you do nothing?' and they say 'Not much', what should you do?
questions.explanation: No pain = No sale. If the status quo is bearable, they will never go through the friction of switching to your product.

questions.question: What is the 'Strip Line' technique?

options.text: Pulling away and suggesting they stick with their current solution
Offering a stripped-down version of the product

questions.explanation: By pulling away ('Maybe you should stick with spreadsheets'), you force the prospect to either agree (Disqualify) or defend their need (Sell themselves).

questions.question: True or False: A direct question will scare away a good prospect.

options.text: False, directness signals professionalism and respect

questions.explanation: High-value prospects are busy. They appreciate directness. Only tire-kickers are 'scared away' by direct questions (which is good).
